trigger: LLM generated async test suite using synchronous test runner patterns without using pytest-asyncio
symptom: Tests pass locally but silently don't test anything; coroutines return immediately as coroutine objects without being awaited; DeprecationWarning on 3.10
bad_code:
# tests/test_creative_reasoning.py (pattern repeated x7)
def test_run_creative_job():
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    result = loop.run_until_complete(run_creative_job('test prompt'))
    assert result['status'] == 'complete'

def test_score_calculation():
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    scores = loop.run_until_complete(calculate_scores(result))
    assert scores['novelty'] > 0
good_code:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_run_creative_job():
    result = await run_creative_job('test prompt')
    assert result['status'] == 'complete'

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_score_calculation():
    scores = await calculate_scores(result)
    assert scores['novelty'] > 0
notes: LLMs trained on pre-asyncio-pytest patterns frequently generate get_event_loop().run_until_complete() in tests. With pytest-asyncio and @pytest.mark.asyncio this is unnecessary and fragile. Add pytest-asyncio to deps and use async def tests.
